subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main theme and message of the poem
Identify the poet’s depiction of nature and trees
Learn simple poetic features like rhyme and repetition
Develop reading comprehension and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Reading
topics_covered: Background of the poet and poem
Theme: Strength and resilience of nature
Introduction to trees as a symbol
teaching_strategy: Teacher reads the poem aloud clearly
Explain difficult words and lines in simple terms
student_activities: Underline unfamiliar words and write meanings in notebook

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Theme
topics_covered: The difficulty of destroying a tree
Tree as a symbol of life and strength
teaching_strategy: Explain stanza-wise meaning in simple language
Give examples from daily life and nature
student_activities: Write the main idea of each stanza in own words

lesson_plan.title: Poetic Features
topics_covered: Simple imagery describing tree and nature
Rhyme and rhythm in selected lines
teaching_strategy: Point out repeated words and images
Discuss their effect on reader’s understanding
student_activities: Explain meaning of simple imagery in own words

lesson_plan.title: Moral and Reflection
teaching_strategy: Discuss moral in simple, relatable terms
Encourage students to share observations from nature
student_activities: Write one sentence about what they learned from the poem
Share examples of strong trees or plants they know

teacher_tip: Keep explanations simple and focus on helping students appreciate the resilience of nature and the moral lesson about protecting trees.
